Items.Title: RxJS 7 - 管道运算符 | RxJS 7 - Pipeable Operators
Items.Description:
这是我在 Jurek Wozniak 创建的名为RxJS 7 and Observables: Introduction 的精彩课程中的详细笔记。

为了理解概念请考虑自己购买课程，这些笔记只是附属品

运营商堆叠

管道运算符在事件实际到达观察者之前进行一些操作。

这里重要的是观察者只能访问所有这些运算符之后的最后一个值。

这就像 javascript 数组实用函数一样工作。

注意：应用管道运算符会创建一个带有一些附加逻辑的新可观察对象，它不会修改现有可观察对象。

筛选

当一个值传递给过滤器函数时，它要么传递给（其他运算符或可观察对象），要么被丢弃。

即使过滤器函数过滤了下一个发出的通知，它总是传递错误和完成

我们可以从任何可观察对象调用 pipe 方法，然后我们可以调用我们想要的任何运算符。

 import { Observable } from 'rxjs'; import { filter } from 'rxjs/operators'; interface NewsItem { category: 'Bussiness' | 'Sports'; content: string; } const newsFeed$ = new Observable<NewsItem>((subscriber) => { setTimeout(() => { subscriber.next({ category: 'Bussiness', content: 'A' }); }, 1000); setTimeout(() => { subscriber.next({ category: 'Sports', content: 'B' }); }, 3000); setTimeout(() => { subscriber.next({ category: 'Bussiness', content: 'C' }); }, 4000); setTimeout(() => { subscriber.next({ category: 'Sports', content: 'D' }); }, 5000); setTimeout(() => { subscriber.next({ category: 'Bussiness', content: 'E' }); }, 6000); }); newsFeed$ .pipe(filter((item) => item.category === 'Sports')) .subscribe((item) => console.log(item));

如果我们愿意，我们可以只提取运动提要并在我们想要的任何地方使用它

import { Observable } from "rxjs"; import { filter } from "rxjs/operators"; interface NewsItem { category: 'Business' | 'Sports'; content: string; } const newsFeed$ = new Observable<NewsItem>(subscriber => { setTimeout(() => subscriber.next({ category: 'Business', content: 'A' }), 1000); setTimeout(() => subscriber.next({ category: 'Sports', content: 'B' }), 3000); setTimeout(() => subscriber.next({ category: 'Business', content: 'C' }), 4000); setTimeout(() => subscriber.next({ category: 'Sports', content: 'D' }), 6000); setTimeout(() => subscriber.next({ category: 'Business', content: 'E' }), 7000); }); const sportsNewsFeed$ = newsFeed$.pipe( filter(item => item.category === 'Sports') ); newsFeed$.subscribe( item => console.log(item) );

地图

轻敲

因为在 map 中，我们返回的值有时会成为下一个链式函数的输入以进行调试，例如我们可以在中间传递一个 tap() 函数并监视正在发生的事情

import { of } from "rxjs"; import { filter, map, tap } from "rxjs/operators"; of(1, 7, 3, 6, 2).pipe( filter(value => value > 5), map(value => value * 2), tap({ next: value => console.log('Spy:', value) }), ).subscribe(value => console.log('Output:', value));

但是当然，如​​果我们使用带有花括号的 map 函数和带有 return 关键字的返回值，就在返回它们之前我们也可以控制台日志。

但请记住，tap 运算符不仅仅是一个控制台日志，它可以产生任何副作用，因此它是一个非常有用的工具。

还有一件事…

从RxJS 7.3.0开始， tap()运算符可以做更多的事情。 您可以在tap()运算符级别查看订阅何时开始和结束。

在我们上面讨论的 'next'、'error' 和 'complete' 处理程序之上， tap()运算符引入了另外三个处理程序：

  • 这不会在发出“错误”或“完成”时执行，
  • 去抖时间

    例如，我们有多个通知，比如说每秒 5 个通知，我们只对最后一个值感兴趣。 当我们监听输入字段时，我们可以有这样的场景，用户快速写入，当用户停下来一点时，我们最终发出值并对其进行处理。

    这样我们就可以避免过多的处理。


     import { fromEvent } from 'rxjs'; import { map, debounceTime } from 'rxjs/operators'; const sliderInput = document.querySelector('input#slider'); fromEvent<any>(sliderInput, 'input') .pipe( debounceTime(500), map((event) => event.target['value']) ) .subscribe((value) => console.log(value));

    在这个例子中，它没有发出数千条通知，而是发出了 3 条通知，这是一个惊人的性能提升。

    捕获错误

    它会自动传递它不感兴趣的 next() 和 complete() 函数。

    如果发生错误通知而不是将其传递给下一个链接函数，它会将其传递给可观察对象。

    这个 observable 可能会直接发出 next() 和 complete() 通知，然后将这些通知传递给下一个链接函数。

     import { Observable, of } from 'rxjs'; import { catchError } from 'rxjs/operators'; const failingHttpRequest$ = new Observable((subscriber) => { setTimeout(() => { subscriber.error(new Error('Timeout')); }, 3000); return () => console.log('Teardown'); }); console.log('App started'); failingHttpRequest$ .pipe( catchError((error) => of(`Fallback value and err message: ${error.message}`) ) ) .subscribe({ next: (value) => console.log(value), error: (err) => console.log(err.message), complete: () => console.log('Completed'), });

    我们看到错误并没有传递给其他链式函数，我们只是用 * of * 创建一个新的可观察对象，并将错误消息也传递给 next() 函数。

    我们还可以看到，在函数发送完成通知之后，它还会向我们的主管道发出完成通知。

    有时我们可能想隐藏错误消息或者在错误发生时不想做任何事情，对于这些情况，rxjs 有一个 ******* EMPTY ******* observable，这个 observable 顾名思义是空的并且只发出 complete() 通知。

     import { EMPTY, Observable, of } from "rxjs"; import { catchError } from "rxjs/operators"; const failingHttpRequest$ = new Observable(subscriber => { setTimeout(() => { subscriber.error(new Error('Timeout')); }, 3000); }); console.log('App started'); failingHttpRequest$.pipe( catchError(error => EMPTY) ).subscribe({ next: value => console.log(value), complete: () => console.log('Completed') });

    扁平化运算符

    展平运算符的工作方式类似于捕获错误，但用于下一次通知。

    展平运算符通过订阅一个新的可观察对象来对下一个通知做出反应，只要源发出新值，展平运算符就会继续订阅新的可观察对象。

    扁平化运算符静态示例

    在展平运算符中，首先源发出一个值，并使用该值创建一个新的可观察对象，并且它也开始发出新值，然后如果我们有来自新创建的可观察对象的完整通知，则它们不会发送到主对象通知。

    让我们用 concatMap 做一个例子

    import { Observable, of } from 'rxjs'; import { concatMap } from 'rxjs/operators'; const source$ = new Observable((subscriber) => { setTimeout(() => subscriber.next('A'), 2000); setTimeout(() => subscriber.next('B'), 3000); }); console.log('App has started'); source$ .pipe(concatMap((value) => of(1, 2))) .subscribe((value) => console.log(value));

    如我们所见，我们将值“A”和“B”完全映射到那些可观察值。

    即使对于静态数字，如果我们可以处理热可观察对象等的创建，它也不是一个真正有效的用例。这些扁平化运算符可能非常有用。

    扁平化运算符 - 动态 HTTP 请求

    这次让我们创建一个 *************** concatMap *************** 示例，它将发送一个包含提供的值的 http 请求

    import { fromEvent } from "rxjs"; import { ajax } from "rxjs/ajax"; import { concatMap, map } from "rxjs/operators"; const endpointInput: HTMLInputElement = document.querySelector('input#endpoint'); const fetchButton = document.querySelector('button#fetch'); fromEvent(fetchButton, 'click').pipe( map(() => endpointInput.value), concatMap(value => ajax(`https://random-data-api.com/api/${value}/random_${value}`) ) ).subscribe( value => console.log(value) );

    扁平化运算符 - 错误处理 #1

    即使完整的通知没有传递到主流，错误通知也会传递！

    所以我们需要小心处理 tem 以免杀死我们的主管道

    import { EMPTY, fromEvent } from "rxjs"; import { ajax } from "rxjs/ajax"; import { catchError, concatMap, map } from "rxjs/operators"; const endpointInput: HTMLInputElement = document.querySelector('input#endpoint'); const fetchButton = document.querySelector('button#fetch'); fromEvent(fetchButton, 'click').pipe( map(() => endpointInput.value), concatMap(value => ajax(`https://random-data-api.com/api/${value}/random_${value}`) ), catchError(() => EMPTY) ).subscribe({ next: value => console.log(value), error: err => console.log('Error:', err), complete: () => console.log('Completed') });

    我们对这种方法有一个问题，即使我们捕获了错误，我们还是在主管道中捕获了它并将其转换为完整的通知，这也杀死了主管道并且我们无法继续搜索。

    扁平化运算符 - 错误处理 #2

    为了解决上述问题，我们需要将 catchError 链接到内部可观察对象

    import { EMPTY, fromEvent, of } from 'rxjs'; import { ajax } from 'rxjs/ajax'; import { catchError, concatMap, map } from 'rxjs/operators'; const endpointInput: HTMLInputElement = document.querySelector('input#endpoint'); const fetchButton = document.querySelector('button#fetch'); fromEvent(fetchButton, 'click') .pipe( map(() => endpointInput.value), concatMap((value) => ajax(`https://random-data-api.com/api/${value}/random_${value}`).pipe( catchError((error) => of(`Could not fetch data: ${error}`)) ) ) ) .subscribe({ next: (value) => console.log(value), error: (err) => console.log('Error:', err), complete: () => console.log('Completed'), });

    扁平化运算符 - 并发 - concatMap

    在第一个可观察对象完成之前，concatMap 运算符不会传递给下一个可观察对象。

    这可能是一个很好的特性，因为未完成的可观察对象会在长期内造成内存泄漏。

    这非常重要（例如考虑多个 httpRequests 或鼠标位置）

    扁平化运算符 - switchMap

    例如，如果在前一个可观察对象仍未提供完整通知时发出了一个新值，则 switchMap 会自动取消前一个可观察对象并使用新值创建新可观察对象。

    它会造成不重要的内存泄漏，因为程序会在取消操作后清理此内存

    这样它就可以阻止内存泄漏，并且可以根据情况非常快速地采取行动。

    展平运算符 - mergeMap

    有时，这正是我们所需要的。

    让我们保持联系

    嘿，如果你读到这里，非常感谢。 我希望你保持联系以获得更多甜蜜的内容。

    请订阅 Dev.to 和其他频道（🦸‍♂️ 尤其是推特）



原文:

Here are my detailed notes from the fantastic course created by Jurek Wozniak, called RxJS 7 and Observables: Introduction.

In order to understand the concept please consider buying the course yourself, these notes are simply subsidiaries

Operator Stacking

Pipe operators make some operations before an event actually reaches to an observer.

[Event Source → operator 1 → operator 2 → operator 3 → …] → observer

Important thing here is observer only have access the last value after all those operators.

This works just like javascript array utility functions.

Note: Applying a pipeable operator creates a new observable with some additional logic, it doesn’t modify the existing observable.

filter

When a value passed to the filter function it will either pass to (other operators or observable) or it will be discarded.

Even though filter function filters notifications emitted by the next, it always passes error and complete

We can call pipe method from any observable and then we can call any operator we want.

import { Observable } from 'rxjs';
import { filter } from 'rxjs/operators'; interface NewsItem { category: 'Bussiness' | 'Sports'; content: string;
} const newsFeed$ = new Observable<NewsItem>((subscriber) => { setTimeout(() => { subscriber.next({ category: 'Bussiness', content: 'A' }); }, 1000); setTimeout(() => { subscriber.next({ category: 'Sports', content: 'B' }); }, 3000); setTimeout(() => { subscriber.next({ category: 'Bussiness', content: 'C' }); }, 4000); setTimeout(() => { subscriber.next({ category: 'Sports', content: 'D' }); }, 5000); setTimeout(() => { subscriber.next({ category: 'Bussiness', content: 'E' }); }, 6000);
}); newsFeed$ .pipe(filter((item) => item.category === 'Sports')) .subscribe((item) => console.log(item));

If we want we can extract only sport feed and use it in anywhere we want

import { Observable } from "rxjs";
import { filter } from "rxjs/operators"; interface NewsItem { category: 'Business' | 'Sports'; content: string;
} const newsFeed$ = new Observable<NewsItem>(subscriber => { setTimeout(() => subscriber.next({ category: 'Business', content: 'A' }), 1000); setTimeout(() => subscriber.next({ category: 'Sports', content: 'B' }), 3000); setTimeout(() => subscriber.next({ category: 'Business', content: 'C' }), 4000); setTimeout(() => subscriber.next({ category: 'Sports', content: 'D' }), 6000); setTimeout(() => subscriber.next({ category: 'Business', content: 'E' }), 7000);
}); const sportsNewsFeed$ = newsFeed$.pipe( filter(item => item.category === 'Sports')
); newsFeed$.subscribe( item => console.log(item)
);

map

map function works really similar to js map function

import { forkJoin } from "rxjs";
// Mike is from New Delhi and likes to eat pasta. import { ajax } from "rxjs/ajax";
import { map } from "rxjs/operators"; const randomFirstName$ = ajax<any>('https://random-data-api.com/api/name/random_name').pipe( map(ajaxResponse => ajaxResponse.response.first_name)
); const randomCapital$ = ajax<any>('https://random-data-api.com/api/nation/random_nation').pipe( map(ajaxResponse => ajaxResponse.response.capital)
); const randomDish$ = ajax<any>('https://random-data-api.com/api/food/random_food').pipe( map(ajaxResponse => ajaxResponse.response.dish)
); forkJoin([randomFirstName$, randomCapital$, randomDish$]).subscribe( ([firstName, capital, dish]) => console.log(`${firstName} is from ${capital} and likes to eat ${dish}.`)
);

tap

***tab*** operator works like a spy and allow us to make some side effect without interacting with the notifications.

Because of in map, the value we return becomes the input of the next chained function sometimes for debug purposes for example we can pass a tap() function in the middle and spy what is happening

import { of } from "rxjs";
import { filter, map, tap } from "rxjs/operators"; of(1, 7, 3, 6, 2).pipe( filter(value => value > 5), map(value => value * 2), tap({ next: value => console.log('Spy:', value) }),
).subscribe(value => console.log('Output:', value));

But of couse if we would use map function with curly brances and return values with return keyword, just before returning them we could also console log.

But keep in mid that tap operator is more than just a console log, it is there for making any side effects therefore it is quite a useful tool.

One more thing…

Starting from RxJS 7.3.0, the tap() operator can do even more. You can see when the Subscription starts and ends at the level of the tap() operator.

On top of the ‘next’, ‘error’ and ‘complete’ handlers which we have discussed above, the tap() operator introduces three more handlers:

  • subscribe — called when a new Subscription is made,
  • unsubscribe — called when the Subscription is closed by unsubscribing (calling unsubscribe); this will NOT be executed when ‘error’ or ‘complete’ is emitted,
  • finalize — called when the Subscription ends, no matter what the cause: it can be either unsubscribing, an error or a complete notification; all will cause the ‘finalize’ handler to be called. 
... tap({ subscribe: () => { console.log('New Subscription'); }, unsubscribe: () => { console.log('Unsubscribed'); }, finalize: () => { console.log('Subscription ended'); } }),
...

debounceTime

*********************debounceTime********************* delays the emitted notifications and only emits the lastly emitted value after a certain threshold.

For example we have multiple notifications coming let’s say 5 notifications per second and we are only interested in with the last value. We can have such a scenario when we listen for input field, the user writes fastly and when the user stops for a bit we finally emit the value and work on it.

In this way we can avoid the excesive processing.


import { fromEvent } from 'rxjs';
import { map, debounceTime } from 'rxjs/operators'; const sliderInput = document.querySelector('input#slider'); fromEvent<any>(sliderInput, 'input') .pipe( debounceTime(500), map((event) => event.target['value']) ) .subscribe((value) => console.log(value));

In this example instead of emitting thousands of notifications it just emits 3, which is an amazing performance gain.

catchError

catchError function provides a fallback observable in case of error. It automatically pass next() and complete() functions it doesn’t interest with them.

If an error notification occurs instead of passing it to the next chained function it passes it to an observable.

And this observable maybe directly emits next() and complete() notifications, then those notifications are passed to the next chained functions.

import { Observable, of } from 'rxjs';
import { catchError } from 'rxjs/operators'; const failingHttpRequest$ = new Observable((subscriber) => { setTimeout(() => { subscriber.error(new Error('Timeout')); }, 3000); return () => console.log('Teardown');
}); console.log('App started'); failingHttpRequest$ .pipe( catchError((error) => of(`Fallback value and err message: ${error.message}`) ) ) .subscribe({ next: (value) => console.log(value), error: (err) => console.log(err.message), complete: () => console.log('Completed'), });

We see that error didn’t pass to the other chained functions instead, we just create a new observable with *of* and passed the next() function also the error message.

We can also see that after of function sends a completed notification it also emits a completed notification to our main pipe.

Sometimes we may want to hide the error message or not want to do anything when error occurs, for those cases rxjs has an *******EMPTY******* observable, this observable as the name suggests is empty and just emits complete() notification.

import { EMPTY, Observable, of } from "rxjs";
import { catchError } from "rxjs/operators"; const failingHttpRequest$ = new Observable(subscriber => { setTimeout(() => { subscriber.error(new Error('Timeout')); }, 3000);
}); console.log('App started'); failingHttpRequest$.pipe( catchError(error => EMPTY)
).subscribe({ next: value => console.log(value), complete: () => console.log('Completed')
});

Flattening Operators

flattening operators work like catch error but for next notification.

Flattening operator react to a next notification by subscribing a new observable, as long as source emit new values flattening operator will keep subscribing to new observables.

Flattening Operators Static Example

In the flattening operators first source emit a value and with that value a new observable is getting created, and it also starts to emit new values, then if we have a complete notification coming from the newly created observable, they are not emitted to the main notifications.

let’s make an example with concatMap

import { Observable, of } from 'rxjs';
import { concatMap } from 'rxjs/operators'; const source$ = new Observable((subscriber) => { setTimeout(() => subscriber.next('A'), 2000); setTimeout(() => subscriber.next('B'), 3000);
}); console.log('App has started');
source$ .pipe(concatMap((value) => of(1, 2))) .subscribe((value) => console.log(value));

As we can see we totally mapped the values ‘A’ and ‘B’ to those observables.

Even though for the static numbers, it is not a really efficient use case, if we can handle creation of hot observables etc. These flattening operators can be quite useful.

Flattening Operators - Dynamic HTTP Request

Let’s this time make a ***************concatMap*************** example that will send an http request with the value provided

import { fromEvent } from "rxjs";
import { ajax } from "rxjs/ajax";
import { concatMap, map } from "rxjs/operators"; const endpointInput: HTMLInputElement = document.querySelector('input#endpoint');
const fetchButton = document.querySelector('button#fetch'); fromEvent(fetchButton, 'click').pipe( map(() => endpointInput.value), concatMap(value => ajax(`https://random-data-api.com/api/${value}/random_${value}`) )
).subscribe( value => console.log(value)
);

Flattening Operators - Error Handling #1

Even though the complete notifications are not passed to the main stream, the error notifications does!

So we need to handle tem carefully in order to not kill our main pipeline

import { EMPTY, fromEvent } from "rxjs";
import { ajax } from "rxjs/ajax";
import { catchError, concatMap, map } from "rxjs/operators"; const endpointInput: HTMLInputElement = document.querySelector('input#endpoint');
const fetchButton = document.querySelector('button#fetch'); fromEvent(fetchButton, 'click').pipe( map(() => endpointInput.value), concatMap(value => ajax(`https://random-data-api.com/api/${value}/random_${value}`) ), catchError(() => EMPTY)
).subscribe({ next: value => console.log(value), error: err => console.log('Error:', err), complete: () => console.log('Completed')
});

We have a problem with this approach, even though we catch the error, we catch it in the main pipeline and convert it to a complete notification this also kills the main pipeline and we cannot keep searching.

Flattening Operators - Error Handling #2

In order to solve the above problem we need to chain catchError to the internal observable

import { EMPTY, fromEvent, of } from 'rxjs';
import { ajax } from 'rxjs/ajax';
import { catchError, concatMap, map } from 'rxjs/operators'; const endpointInput: HTMLInputElement = document.querySelector('input#endpoint');
const fetchButton = document.querySelector('button#fetch'); fromEvent(fetchButton, 'click') .pipe( map(() => endpointInput.value), concatMap((value) => ajax(`https://random-data-api.com/api/${value}/random_${value}`).pipe( catchError((error) => of(`Could not fetch data: ${error}`)) ) ) ) .subscribe({ next: (value) => console.log(value), error: (err) => console.log('Error:', err), complete: () => console.log('Completed'), });

Flattening Operators - Concurrency - concatMap

concatMap operators do not pass to the next observable until the first observable completes.

This can be a good feature since uncompleted observables would create a memory leak in long term.

concatMap always guarantees that observables will be executed in the entered order. And this is super important (think multiple httpRequests or mouse positions for example)

Flattening Operators - switchMap

switchMap provides us almost the same solution as the concatMap but with one big difference.

If for example a new value is emitted while the previous observable still didn’t provide complete notification, switchMap automatically cancels the previous observable and creates the new observable with the new value.

It creates unimportant memory leaks because program cleans this memory after the cancel operation

This way it stops memory leaks and also can act really fast, based on the situation.

Flattening Operators - mergeMap

mergeMap is also like concatMap, but it allows multiple open observables, it doesn’t cancel the previous observable like switchMap, it allows them to work concurrently,

and this is sometimes, the exact thing we need.

Let's keep in touch

Hey, thanks a lot if you read it so far. I want you to keep in touch for more sweet content.

Items.Title: 如何优化 Strapi 支持的网站和应用程序的性能 | How to Optimize Performance of Strapi-Powered Websites and Applications
Items.Description:
斯特拉皮

发布于 3 月 29 日 • 最初发布于strapi.io

性能优化是任何网站或应用程序的重要方面，对于 Strapi 支持的项目尤为重要。 一个优化良好的 Strapi 网站或应用程序将快速加载，快速响应用户交互，并提供流畅无缝的用户体验。

在本文中，您将了解在优化基于 Strapi 的网站或应用程序的性能时需要关注的关键领域。

为什么性能优化对于 Strapi 支持的网站和应用程序很重要？

出于多种原因，性能优化很重要。 一些关键原因包括：

  1. 用户体验：加载缓慢的网站或应用程序会使用户感到沮丧，并可能导致他们离开该网站。 优化性能可以改善整体用户体验。
  2. 搜索引擎优化：谷歌等搜索引擎将网站性能视为排名因素。 更快的网站更有可能在搜索结果中排名更高。
  3. 转化率：更快的网站可以带来更高的转化率，因为如果网站加载速度快，用户更有可能完成购买或注册服务。

在优化基于 Strapi 的网站或应用程序的性能时，需要关注几个关键领域。 这些包括：

  1. 服务器端性能：这包括优化 Node.js 服务器的性能、管理数据库性能以及最小化 API 调用次数。
  2. 前端性能：这包括减少前端加载资产的大小和数量，提高应用程序的感知性能，以及处理图像和媒体资产。
  3. 监控和衡量绩效：这包括使用工具和技术来监控和衡量绩效、分析绩效数据以及设定绩效目标和指标。

优化 Strapi 服务器端性能

优化 Strapi 支持的网站或应用程序服务器端的性能对于确保流畅无缝的用户体验至关重要。 以下是一些在 Strapi 中优化服务器端性能的技术。

  1. 使用反向代理：反向代理，例如 Nginx，可以处理负载均衡和缓存等任务，这可以显着提高 Node.js 服务器的性能。 处理这些任务还可以减少 Node.js 服务器的负载，使其能够处理更多请求并缩短响应时间。
  2. 使用缓存机制：缓存可以通过减少对服务器的请求数量和缩短响应时间来显着提高 Strapi 支持的网站或应用程序的性能。 您还可以使用像 Redis 这样的缓存机制。
  3. 使用内容分发网络 (CDN) ：CDN 可以通过缓存图像和文件等资产并将其从离用户较近的服务器分发给用户，从而帮助减少服务器负载。 这还可以为用户缩短网站或应用程序的加载时间，从而带来更好的用户体验。

管理数据库性能的最佳实践

  1. 使用索引：索引可以让数据库快速定位到需要的数据，从而提高数据库查询的性能。 这可以显着提高网站或应用程序的响应时间。
  2. 使用连接池：连接池允许多个客户端共享有限数量的数据库连接，这可以提高性能并减少数据库的负载。 这也有助于防止数据库成为网站或应用程序性能的瓶颈。
  3. 使用分页：分页可以通过限制单个请求中需要返回的数据量来提高数据库查询的性能。 这也有助于减少数据库的负载并改善网站或应用程序的响应时间。

最小化 API 调用次数和缩短响应时间的技术

  1. 使用 GraphQL ：GraphQL 是一种查询语言，它允许客户端只请求他们需要的数据，可以减少 API 调用次数并提高性能。 这也有助于减少服务器的负载并改善网站或应用程序的响应时间。
  2. 使用缓存：缓存可以显着减少 API 调用次数并提高性能。 这也有助于减少服务器的负载并改善网站或应用程序的响应时间。
  3. 使用压缩：在通过网络发送数据之前压缩数据可以减少需要传输的数据量，从而提高性能。 这也有助于减少服务器的负载并改善网站或应用程序的响应时间

优化前端性能

优化 Strapi 支持的网站或应用程序的前端性能对于确保流畅无缝的用户体验至关重要。

减少前端加载资产的大小和数量的策略

  1. 代码拆分：代码拆分允许开发人员将他们的 JavaScript 代码拆分成更小的块，这些块可以按需加载，从而减少了最初需要加载的代码量。 这可以缩短网站或应用程序的加载时间并减少需要传输的数据量。
  2. 延迟加载：延迟加载允许开发人员仅在需要时加载图像和其他资产，这可以减少初始需要加载的数据量。 这可以缩短网站或应用程序的加载时间并减少需要传输的数据量。
  3. 最小化请求数：最小化对服务器的请求数可以通过减少需要传输的数据量来提高性能。 这可以通过使用缓存和压缩等技术来实现。

提高应用程序感知性能的技术

  1. 骨架屏幕：骨架屏幕可以通过在加载内容时向用户提供视觉反馈来提高应用程序的感知性能。 这可以通过减少感知等待时间来帮助改善用户体验。
  2. 进度指示器：进度指示器可以通过向用户提供有关任务进度的视觉反馈来提高应用程序的感知性能。 这可以通过提供任务状态的透明度来帮助改善用户体验。
  3. 预加载：预加载可以通过加载在不久的将来可能需要的资源来提高应用程序的感知性能。 这可以通过减少加载资源所需的时间来帮助改善用户体验。

在前端处理图像和媒体资产的最佳实践

  1. 使用 webp 格式：WebP 是一种现代图像格式，可以显着减小图像的大小，提高性能。
  2. 使用图像优化工具：图像优化工具可以在不影响图像质量的情况下减小图像的文件大小，从而提高性能。
  3. 使用延迟加载：延迟加载图像可以减少初始需要加载的数据量，从而提高性能。 这也有助于减少对服务器的请求数量，进一步提高性能。

通过实施这些策略和最佳实践，开发人员可以显着提高 Strapi 支持的网站或应用程序的前端性能。 这可以带来更快的加载时间、更好的用户体验以及更多的网站或应用程序参与度。 此外，尽量减少请求数量和减少传输的数据量也有助于降低服务器负载并提高整体性能。

监控和衡量绩效

监视和测量性能是优化 Strapi 支持的网站或应用程序性能的重要步骤。

用于监控和衡量绩效的工具和技术

  1. 性能监控工具：各种性能监控工具，如Google Analytics、New Relic、LogRocket等，都可以监控和衡量性能。 这些工具提供有关网站或应用程序性能的详细信息，包括响应时间、请求数量和错误率。
  2. 浏览器开发工具：Chrome DevTools 和 Firefox DevTools 等浏览器开发工具也可以监控和测量性能。 这些工具提供有关前端性能的信息，包括资产加载时间、网络请求和 JavaScript 执行时间。

分析性能数据并识别瓶颈

  1. 识别慢速页面：识别慢速页面有助于查明需要改进的网站或应用程序区域。
  2. 识别慢速请求：识别慢速请求有助于查明网站或应用程序中需要改进的特定区域。
  3. 识别错误：识别错误有助于查明需要改进的网站或应用程序的特定区域。

设定绩效目标的最佳实践

  1. 设定切合实际的目标：为性能设定切合实际的目标有助于确保网站或应用程序满足用户的期望。
  2. 跟踪进度：跟踪进度有助于确保网站或应用程序达到既定目标。
  3. 持续优化：优化网站或应用程序有助于确保最佳性能。

通过实施这些技术，开发人员可以监控和衡量其基于 Strapi 的网站或应用程序的性能，识别瓶颈，并设定性能目标和目标，以确保网站或应用程序始终以最佳性能运行。

结论

总之，性能优化对于任何由 Strapi 支持的网站或应用程序都是必不可少的。 通过关注服务器端性能、前端性能以及监控和测量性能等关键领域，开发人员可以改善整体用户体验并提高 Strapi 项目的效率。

服务器端性能优化技术（例如使用反向代理、缓存和连接池）可以帮助减少服务器负载并缩短响应时间。

前端性能优化策略（例如减少加载资产的大小和数量、提高感知性能以及处理图像和媒体资产）有助于改善用户体验。

使用工具和技术监控和测量性能、设置性能目标以及遵循最佳实践对于识别瓶颈和确保网站或应用程序的性能达到标准也很重要。

最后，通过遵循本文概述的技术和最佳实践，开发人员可以显着提高其 Strapi 网站和应用程序的性能，从而获得更高效和用户友好的体验。

原文:

Strapi

Posted on Mar 29 • Originally published at strapi.io

Performance optimization is an essential aspect of any website or application and is particularly important for Strapi-powered projects. A well-optimized Strapi website or application will load quickly, respond quickly to user interactions, and provide a smooth and seamless user experience.

In this article, you will learn about the key areas to focus on when optimizing the performance of a Strapi-powered website or application.

Why is Performance Optimization Important for Strapi-powered Websites and Applications?

Performance optimization is important for many reasons. Some of the key reasons include the following:

  1. User experience: A slow-loading website or application can frustrate users and may cause them to leave the site. Optimizing performance can improve the overall user experience.
  2. Search engine optimization: Search engines like Google consider website performance as a ranking factor. A faster website is more likely to rank higher in search results.
  3. Conversion rates: A faster website can lead to higher conversion rates, as users are more likely to complete a purchase or sign up for a service if the site loads quickly.

Key Areas to Focus on for Performance Optimization in Strapi

When optimizing the performance of a Strapi-powered website or application, there are several key areas to focus on. These include:

  1. Server-side performance: This includes optimizing the performance of the Node.js server, managing database performance, and minimizing the number of API calls.
  2. Front-end performance: This includes reducing the size and number of assets loaded on the front-end, improving the perceived performance of the application, and handling image and media assets.
  3. Monitoring and measuring performance: This includes using tools and techniques to monitor and measure performance, analyzing performance data, and setting performance goals and targets.

Optimizing Strapi Server-side Performance

Optimizing the performance of the server side of a Strapi-powered website or application is crucial for ensuring a smooth and seamless user experience. Here are some techniques for optimizing server-side performance in Strapi.

  1. Use a reverse proxy: A reverse proxy, such as Nginx, can handle tasks such as load balancing and caching, which can significantly improve the performance of the Node.js server. Handling these tasks can also reduce the load on the Node.js server, allowing it to handle more requests and improve response times.
  2. Use a caching mechanism: Caching can significantly improve the performance of a Strapi-powered website or application by reducing the number of requests to the server and improving response times. Strapi has built-in caching capabilities; you can also use a caching mechanism like Redis.
  3. Use a Content Delivery Network (CDN): A CDN can help reduce the server load by caching and delivering assets such as images and files to users from servers that are closer to them. This can also improve the loading time of the website or application for users, leading to a better user experience.

Best Practices for Managing Database Performance

  1. Use indexes: Indexes can improve the performance of database queries by allowing the database to quickly locate the required data. This can significantly improve the response time of the website or application.
  2. Use connection pooling: Connection pooling allows multiple clients to share a limited number of database connections, which can improve performance and reduce the load on the database. This can also help to prevent the database from becoming a bottleneck in the performance of the website or application.
  3. Use pagination: Pagination can improve the performance of database queries by limiting the amount of data that needs to be returned in a single request. This can also help to reduce the load on the database and improve the response time of the website or application.

Techniques for Minimizing the Number of API Calls and Reducing Response Times

  1. Use GraphQL: GraphQL is a query language that allows clients to request only the data they need, which can reduce the number of API calls and improve performance. This can also help to reduce the load on the server and improve the response time of the website or application.
  2. Use caching: Caching can significantly reduce the number of API calls and improve performance. This can also help to reduce the load on the server and improve the response time of the website or application.
  3. Use compression: Compressing data before sending it over the network can reduce the amount of data that needs to be transferred, improving performance. This can also help to reduce the load on the server and improve the response time of the website or application

Optimizing Front-end Performance

Optimizing the performance of the front-end of a Strapi-powered website or application is crucial for ensuring a smooth and seamless user experience.

Strategies for Reducing the Size and Number of Assets Loaded on the Front-end

  1. Code splitting: Code splitting allows developers to split their JavaScript code into smaller chunks, which can be loaded on demand, reducing the amount of code that needs to be loaded initially. This can improve the loading time of the website or application and reduce the amount of data that needs to be transferred.
  2. Lazy loading: Lazy loading allows developers to load images and other assets only when they are needed, which can reduce the amount of data that needs to be loaded initially. This can improve the loading time of the website or application and reduce the amount of data that needs to be transferred.
  3. Minimizing the number of requests: Minimizing the number of requests to the server can improve performance by reducing the amount of data that needs to be transferred. This can be achieved by using techniques such as caching and compression.

Techniques for Improving the Perceived Performance of the Application

  1. Skeleton screens: Skeleton screens can improve the perceived performance of an application by providing visual feedback to the user while the content is loading. This can help to improve the user experience by reducing the perceived waiting time.
  2. Progress indicators: Progress indicators can improve the perceived performance of an application by providing visual feedback to the user about the progress of a task. This can help improve the user experience by providing transparency on the task status.
  3. Preloading: Preloading can improve the perceived performance of an application by loading resources that are likely to be needed in the near future. This can help to improve the user experience by reducing the time it takes for the resources to be loaded.

Best Practices for Handling Image and Media Assets on the Front-end

  1. Use webp format: WebP is a modern image format that can significantly reduce the size of images, improving performance.
  2. Use image optimization tools: Image optimization tools can reduce the file size of images without affecting their quality, improving performance.
  3. Use lazy loading: Lazy loading images can reduce the amount of data that needs to be loaded initially, improving performance. This can also help to reduce the number of requests to the server, further improving performance.

By implementing these strategies and best practices, developers can significantly improve the performance of the front-end of a Strapi-powered website or application. This can lead to faster loading times, a better user experience, and increased engagement with the website or application. In addition, minimizing the number of requests and reducing the amount of data transferred can also help reduce the server load and improve overall performance.

Monitoring and Measuring Performance

Monitoring and measuring performance is an important step in optimizing the performance of a Strapi-powered website or application.

Tools and Techniques for Monitoring and Measuring Performance

  1. Performance monitoring tools: Various performance monitoring tools, such as Google Analytics, New Relic, and LogRocket, can monitor and measure performance. These tools provide detailed information on the performance of the website or application, including response times, number of requests, and error rates.
  2. Browser developer tools: Browser developer tools such as Chrome DevTools and Firefox DevTools can also monitor and measure performance. These tools provide information on the performance of the front-end, including the loading time of assets, network requests, and JavaScript execution time.

Analyzing Performance Data and Identifying Bottlenecks

  1. Identify slow pages: Identifying slow pages can help to pinpoint areas of the website or application that need improvement.
  2. Identify slow requests: Identifying slow requests can help to pinpoint specific areas of the website or application that need improvement.
  3. Identify errors: Identifying errors can help to pinpoint specific areas of the website or application that need improvement.

Best practices for setting performance goals and targets

  1. Set realistic goals: Setting realistic goals for performance can help ensure that the website or application meets the users' expectations.
  2. Track progress: Tracking progress can help ensure the website or application meets the set goals and targets.
  3. Continuously optimize: Optimizing the website or application can help ensure optimal performance.

With the implementation of these techniques, developers can monitor and measure the performance of their Strapi-powered website or application, identify bottlenecks, and set performance goals and targets to ensure that the website or application is always running at optimal performance.

Conclusion

In conclusion, performance optimization is essential to any Strapi-powered website or application. By focusing on key areas such as server-side performance, front-end performance, and monitoring and measuring performance, developers can improve the overall user experience and increase the efficiency of their Strapi projects.

Server-side performance optimization techniques such as using a reverse proxy, caching, and connection pooling can help reduce the server load and improve response times.

Front-end performance optimization strategies such as reducing the size and number of assets loaded, improving perceived performance, and handling images and media assets can help to improve the user experience.

Monitoring and measuring performance using tools and techniques, setting performance goals, and following best practices are also important for identifying bottlenecks and ensuring that the performance of the website or application is up to the standard.

Finally, by following the techniques and best practices outlined in this article, developers can significantly improve the performance of their Strapi websites and applications, resulting in a more efficient and user-friendly experience.

在本博文系列的第 1 部分中，我们讨论了在学习 ReactJS 之前需要掌握的基本要求。

现在，我们将专注于熟悉 ReactJS 语法。

类到类名

将类分配给 HTML 标签时，我们使用className而不是 ReactJS 中的class 。 原因是因为在 JavaScript 中， class是一个特殊的关键字，并且由于 React 应用程序 (JSX) 是基于 JavaScript 的，所以使用 className 来避免任何命名冲突。

例如：

 <div className="my-class-name"></div>

导入和导出

在构建 Web 应用程序时使用像 Reactjs 这样的库的最好原因之一是因为具有可重用组件的特点......您可以创建一个组件，比方说 Navbar 并为您的整个应用程序使用相同的代码段。

这怎么可能？ 通过导入和导出组件，我们可以导入模块、图像等。

这是一个例子：

 import React from 'react'; function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <h1>Hello World!</h1> </div> ); } export default App;

在这里，我们从名为react的源导入“REACT”包，以便我们可以使用基础代码库来构建我们的应用程序。

然后我们导出组件，以便我们也可以在其他地方使用它。

反应挂钩

这些是可重用的函数，可以在 ReactJS 中管理和操作状态和生命周期。

在 React v16.8 之前，开发人员使用类方法来声明组件，但是随着项目变大，语法很难理解。 现在，几乎所有 React 开发人员都使用函数式组件而不是基于类的组件声明。

两个最常用的 React Hooks 是useState和useEffect 。 存在其他 Hooks，开发人员也可以通过扩展 React Hooks 创建他们的 hooks。

有了这些知识，让我们构建一个简单的 ReactJS 应用程序。

构建一个简单的 ReactJS 应用程序

我们的简单应用程序将具有一个表单页面，该页面获取用户数据并在用户单击提交按钮时将结果记录到控制台。 由于我们没有后端端点，因此我们不会将数据提交到服务器。

以下是我们需要做的事情的概述：

  • 引导一个 React 项目
  • 清理启动文件
  • 创建表单

让我们开始吧！

第 1 步：引导一个 React 项目

要引导 React 项目，我们将使用create-next-app 。 打开终端，导航到要创建项目的目录，然后运行以下命令：

 npx create-next-app my-app-name

这将创建一个名为 my-app-name 的新 React 项目。 接下来，导航到项目目录并使用以下命令启动开发服务器：

注意：您可以使用任何 Reactjs 框架来构建 React 应用程序，例如 Next、Vite 等。我们在本教程中使用Nextjs 。

现在您的应用程序已启动并运行，您可以在浏览器中访问localhost:3000/ 。

步骤 2. 清理启动文件

在我们创建表单之前，让我们清理启动文件。 删除src目录中除 index.js 和 App.js 之外的所有内容。 我们还将删除 App.js 的内容。

我们的 App.js 文件应该是这样的：

 import React from 'react'; function App() { return ( <div className="App"> </div> ); } export default App;

另外，不要忘记删除所有已删除文件的导入，否则，您的应用程序将抛出错误，因为您正在导入不再存在的文件。

您可以尝试搜索在代码编辑器中删除的文件和文件夹的名称，并删除所有导入。

如果你使用的是Vs code ，它会警告你并告诉你那些死进口在哪里。

步骤 3. 创建表单

让我们在App.js文件中添加一个表单。 我们将使用useState钩子为表单输入值创建一个状态。 这是我们的 App.js 文件的完整代码：

 import React, { useState } from 'react'; function Form() { const [name, setName] = useState(''); const [email, setEmail] = useState(''); const [phone, setPhone] = useState(''); function handleSubmit(event) { event.preventDefault(); console.log(`Name: ${name}\nEmail: ${email}\nPhone: ${phone}`); } return ( <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}> <label> Name: <input type="text" value={name} onChange={event => setName(event.target.value)} /> </label> <br /> <label> Email: <input type="email" value={email} onChange={event => setEmail(event.target.value)} /> </label> <br /> <label> Phone: <input type="tel" value={phone} onChange={event => setPhone(event.target.value)} /> </label> <br /> <button type="submit">Submit</button> </form> ); } export default Form;

代码解释：

在上面的代码中，我们使用useState钩子来管理表单输入的状态（或存储初始值）。 我们还定义了一个名为handleSubmit的函数，该函数将在提交表单时调用。

此函数将阻止默认的表单提交行为，例如重新加载表单页面（我们为此目的使用event.preventDefault() ）并将表单数据记录到控制台。

此外，我们正在处理<input />的更改以收听任何用户类型。 如果您不处理表单上的更改，您将不会从用户那里获得输入值。

除了 handleChange，还有其他方法可以从表单中获取价值，但我不会在这篇博文中离题

另外，请注意，使用纯 JavaScript，您将必须获取特定元素（例如按钮）的 ID，并像这样对其应用addEventListener ：

 const button = document.querySelector(“.btn”) button.addEvenListener(“click” function())

在reactjs中对此

<button onClick={() => function()} />

在 return 语句中，我们使用 JSX 来定义我们的表单结构。 我们使用 value 属性来绑定输入的状态，并使用 onChange 事件在它们发生变化时更新它们的状态。

现在，我们需要将 Form 组件导入到 App.js 文件中，并将其呈现在我们之前创建的 div 元素中。

 import React from 'react'; import './App.css'; import Form from './Form'; function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <h1>My React App</h1> <Form /> </div> ); } export default App;

步骤 3. 测试和部署

现在我们的表单页面已经完成，我们可以通过使用以下命令运行我们的应用程序来测试它：

这将在localhost:3000/打开一个新的浏览器窗口，我们的应用程序正在运行。

在表单中输入一些数据，然后单击提交按钮。 您应该看到记录到控制台的表单数据。

恭喜，你已经构建了你的第一个 React 应用程序！

结论：React.js 是一个简单的前端库

在本教程中，我们学习了 Reactjs 语法的基础知识，包括分配类、导入和导出以及 React Hooks。 我们还构建了一个简单的表单页面，用于获取用户数据并将其记录到控制台。

还有很多东西需要学习，但希望本教程为您提供了一个良好的基础，可以开始构建您自己的 React 应用程序。

如果您有任何问题，请随时提出，我和这里的社区将随时为您提供帮助。

谢谢。



原文:

In Part 1 of this blog post series, we discussed the fundamental requirements to master before learning ReactJS.

Now, we'll focus on getting familiar with the ReactJS syntax.

class to className

When assigning a class to an HTML tag, we use className instead of class in ReactJS. And the reason is because in JavaScript, class is a special keyword and since react app (JSX) is JavaScript-based, className is used to avoid any naming conflict.

For example:

<div className="my-class-name"></div>

Importing and exporting

One of the best reasons why libraries like Reactjs is used in building web application is because of reusable component featured… You can create a component, let’s say Navbar and use the same piece of code for your entire application.

How is this possible? By importing and exporting the component and we can import modules, images etc.

Here's an example:

import React from 'react'; function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <h1>Hello World!</h1>
 </div>
 );
} export default App;

Here we are importing “REACT” package from a source called react so that we can use the foundational code base to build our application.

And then we are exporting the component so that we can use it somewhere else as well.

React Hooks

Another essential feature of ReactJS is React Hooks.

These are reusable functions that give access to managing and manipulating states and lifecycles in ReactJS.

Before React v16.8, developers used a class method for declaring components, but it was difficult to understand the syntax as the project got larger. Now, almost all React developers use functional components over class-based component declarations.

The two most commonly used React Hooks are useState and useEffect. Other Hooks exist, and developers can also create their hooks by extending React Hooks.

With this knowledge, let's build a simple ReactJS application.

Building a Simple ReactJS Application

Our simple app will feature a form page that takes user data and logs the result to the console when the user clicks the submit button. Since we don't have a backend endpoint, we won't submit the data to the server.

Here's an overview of what we need to do:

  • Bootstrap a React project
  • Clean up the starter files
  • Create the form

Let's get started!

Step 1: Bootstrap a React Project

To bootstrap a React project, we'll use create-next-app. Open your terminal, navigate to the directory where you want to create your project, and run the following command:

npx create-next-app my-app-name

This will create a new React project named my-app-name. Next, navigate to the project directory and start the development server with the following command:

NOTE: You can use any Reactjs framework to build react application such as Next, Vite etc. We are using Nextjs in this tutorial.

Now that your app is up and running, you can it in your browser at localhost:3000/.

Step 2. Clean Up the Starter Files

Before we create the form, let's clean up the starter files. Delete everything in the src directory except for index.js and App.js. We'll also remove the contents of App.js.

Our App.js file should look like this:

import React from 'react'; function App() { return ( <div className="App"> </div>
 );
} export default App;

Also, don't forget to remove all the imports of the files deleted otherwise, your app will throw error because you're importing files that no longer exist.

You can try searching for the names of the files and folders deleted in your code editor and remove all the imports.

If you're using Vs code, it will warn you and tell you where those dead imports are.

Step 3. Create the Form

Let's add a form to our App.js file. We'll create a state for the form input values using useState hook. Here's the complete code for our App.js file:

import React, { useState } from 'react'; function Form() { const [name, setName] = useState(''); const [email, setEmail] = useState(''); const [phone, setPhone] = useState(''); function handleSubmit(event) { event.preventDefault(); console.log(`Name: ${name}\nEmail: ${email}\nPhone: ${phone}`); } return ( <form onSubmit={handleSubmit}> <label> Name: <input type="text" value={name} onChange={event => setName(event.target.value)} />
 </label>
 <br /> <label> Email: <input type="email" value={email} onChange={event => setEmail(event.target.value)} />
 </label>
 <br /> <label> Phone: <input type="tel" value={phone} onChange={event => setPhone(event.target.value)} />
 </label>
 <br /> <button type="submit">Submit</button>
 </form>
 );
} export default Form;

Code explained:

In the code above, we are using the useState hook to manage the state (or store the initial value) of our form inputs. We are also defining a function called handleSubmit that will be called when the form is submitted.

This function will prevent the default form submission behavior such as reloading the form page (we use event.preventDefault() fot that purpose) and log the form data to the console.

Also we are handling change on our <input /> to listen to whatever user type. If you don't handle change on forms, the you will not get input value from the user.

There are other ways to get value from form aside handleChange but I won't digress to that in this blog post

Also, notice that with pure JavaScript, you will have to get the id of a particular element (say button) and apply addEventListener to it like this:

const button = document.querySelector(“.btn”) button.addEvenListener(“click” function())

to this in reactjs

<button onClick={() => function()} />

Inside the return statement, we are using JSX to define our form structure. We are using the value attribute to bind the state of our inputs and the onChange event to update their state when they change.

Now, we need to import our Form component into our App.js file and render it inside the div element we created earlier.

import React from 'react';
import './App.css';
import Form from './Form'; function App() { return ( <div className="App"> <h1>My React App</h1>
 <Form /> </div>
 );
} export default App;

Step 3. Test and deploy

Now that our form page is complete, we can test it by running our application with the following command:

This will open up a new browser window at localhost:3000/ with our application running.

Enter some data into the form and click the submit button. You should see the form data logged to the console.

Congratulations, you have built your first React application!

Conclusion: React.js is a simple frontend library

In this tutorial, we learned the basics of Reactjs syntax, including assigning classes, importing and exporting, and React Hooks. We also built a simple form page that takes user data and logs it to the console.

Reactjs is a powerful library for building web applications, and this tutorial is just the beginning. There is much more to learn, but hopefully, this tutorial has given you a good foundation to start building your own React applications.

If you have any question, feel free to ask and I together with the community here will be ready to help.

Thank you.

在当今的数字世界中，电子邮件已成为个人和专业目的最常用的通信方式之一。 随着电子邮件的使用越来越多，了解可用的不同电子邮件协议及其各自的优缺点非常重要。 在本文中，我们将比较两种最常用的电子邮件协议： POP 和 IMAP。

这意味着一旦下载了电子邮件，它就只能在用户的设备上使用，不能从其他设备访问。 由于电子邮件被下载到用户的设备上，因此可以快速轻松地访问它们。 此外，由于电子邮件一旦下载就会从服务器中删除，因此用户无需担心超过服务器上的电子邮件存储限制。

但是，使用 POP3 也有一些缺点。 由于电子邮件仅在下载它们的设备上可用，因此用户无法从其他设备访问他们的电子邮件。 对于需要在智能手机、平板电脑或台式电脑等多种设备上访问电子邮件的人来说，这可能是一个主要缺点。 此外，由于电子邮件一旦下载就会从服务器中删除，因此用户的电子邮件没有备份，以防他们的设备丢失或被盗。

In today's digital world, email has become one of the most commonly used modes of communication for personal and professional purposes. With the increasing use of email, it's important to understand the different email protocols available and their respective benefits and drawbacks. In this article, we will compare two of the most commonly used email protocols: POP and IMAP.

POP3 (Post Office Protocol version 3) is an email protocol that has been in use since the early days of email. POP3 works by downloading emails from a mail server to the user's device, and then deleting them from the server. This means that once an email is downloaded, it is only available on the user's device and cannot be accessed from another device. POP3 is widely supported by email clients and is easy to set up.

One of the major benefits of POP3 is that it is fast and efficient. Since emails are downloaded to the user's device, they can be accessed quickly and easily. Additionally, since emails are deleted from the server once they are downloaded, users don't need to worry about exceeding their email storage limit on the server.

However, there are some drawbacks to using POP3. Since emails are only available on the device they were downloaded to, users cannot access their emails from other devices. This can be a major drawback for people who need to access their emails on multiple devices, such as a smartphone, tablet, or desktop computer. Additionally, since emails are deleted from the server once they are downloaded, there is no backup of the user's emails in case their device is lost or stolen.

Items.Description:
我们将在本博客中关注各种生命周期方法的 Hooks 实现。

如果您不熟悉状态和生命周期，请查看官方文档。
反应状态和生命周期。 简而言之，如果您想在安装或卸载组件时或在组件的每次渲染时执行函数，这些方法可能很有用。

但是我们不能在钩子中使用任何现有的生命周期方法（componentDidMount、componentDidUpdate、componentWillUnmount 等）。 它们只能在类组件中使用。 而使用 Hooks，你只能在功能组件中使用。 下面这行来自 React 文档：

建议是，您可以从功能组件中的类组件模仿这些生命周期方法。

组件已挂载

此行为的 useEffect 挂钩等效项是

useEffect(() => { // Your code here }, []);

注意这里的第二个参数（空数组）。 这只会运行一次。

如果没有第二个参数，useEffect 钩子将在组件的每次渲染时被调用。

 useEffect(() => { // Your code here });

组件将卸载

假设您要在 componentDidMount 中添加一个事件侦听器，并在 componentWillUnmount 中将其删除，如下所示。

 componentDidMount() { window.addEventListener('mousemove', () => {}) } componentWillUnmount() { window.removeEventListener('mousemove', () => {}) }

与上述代码等效的挂钩如下

useEffect(() => { window.addEventListener('mousemove', () => {}); // returned function will be called on component unmount return () => { window.removeEventListener('mousemove', () => {}) } }, [])

原文:

React State and Lifecycle are very useful methods and with the advancement of React hooks and when a developer uses hooks instead of traditional React classes the most important question becomes how one is gonna implement the lifecycle methods offered by React classes in Hooks. We will look after the Hooks implementation of various lifecycle methods in this blog.

If you are new to state and lifecycle, Have a look at the official docs.
React State and Lifecycle. Briefly, these are the methods which can be useful if you wanna execute a function when a component is mounted or unmounted or at every render of the component.

But we cannot use any of the existing lifecycle methods (componentDidMount, componentDidUpdate, componentWillUnmount etc.) in a hook. They can only be used in class components. And with Hooks, you can only use in functional components. The line below comes from the React doc:

suggest is, you can mimic these lifecycle methods from a class component in a functional component.

ComponentDidMount

Code inside componentDidMount runs once when the component is mounted. useEffect hook equivalent for this behaviour is

useEffect(() => {
  // Your code here
}, []);

Notice the second parameter here (empty array). This will run only once.

ComponentDidUpdate

Without the second parameter the useEffect hook will be called on every render of the component.

useEffect(() => {
  // Your code here
});

ComponentWillUnmount

componentWillUnmount is used for cleanup (like removing event listeners, cancel the timer etc). Say you are adding an event listener in componentDidMount and removing it in componentWillUnmount as below.

componentDidMount() {
  window.addEventListener('mousemove', () => {})
}

componentWillUnmount() {
  window.removeEventListener('mousemove', () => {})
}

Hooks equivalent of the above code will be as follows

useEffect(() => {
  window.addEventListener('mousemove', () => {});

  // returned function will be called on component unmount
  return () => {
    window.removeEventListener('mousemove', () => {})
  }
}, [])

Hope this was helpful 😄

Items.Title: CRM在医疗保健领域的优势 | CRM's Advantages in Healthcare
Items.Description:
所有类型的企业对客户关系管理 (CRM) 软件解决方案的需求都在增加。 各种规模的企业，无论大小，以及非营利组织，都使用它们来改善服务和加强协作。 有关客户和交互的信息通过 CRM 软件进行组织，所有业务流程都得到简化。
可以类似地处理医疗保健。 总的来说，医院正在添加多功能 CRM 系统来解决患者和医生面临的各种问题。 医院与商业企业有着不同的需求，但总的来说，他们正在实施 CRM 系统来服务于各种目的。

需要考虑的方面
由于采用 CRM 软件，医院能够实施更成功的营销活动。

  1. 整合来自整个医疗机构的数据。
  2. 分析数据库以获得最佳营销机会和实施方法。
  3. 衡量活动的投资回报率 (ROI)。

主要优势
跟进患者的活动
感谢现代技术，医疗机构现在正处于成功的患者随访时代。 这条消息的特别之处在于，虽然它不像电话那样私密，但它对医院来说更方便，也更有可能在最重要的时候联系到病人。 此外，外界的照顾也由此得到了有力保障。

与个别患者的沟通

患者可以使用聊天来提供有关他们当前投诉的信息，跟踪他们的约会，并在必要时请求更改。

这些聊天、消息和约会可以链接到主仪表板，提供对有关病史、与医生的关系、处方等信息的完整访问。

与医院管理层的沟通

他们可以访问患者信息，这些信息对于理解新问题和得出每次就诊的结论至关重要。 这些信息包括其他医生的医学测试、诊断和以前的治疗。 公司与医生之间的关系（B2P）

医院还使用现代 CRM 软件与有影响力的医生保持长期关系，这些医生不再是团队的一员，但仍愿意为患者服务。 除了存储患者和内部医生数据的数据库外，该软件还可以为该领域的主要参与者建立一个专用的存储库，以改善 B2P 通信。

提高效率的方法

使用此工具，医疗保健组织可以监控实际结果并评估旨在吸引新患者或提高公众对严重疾病的认识的特定营销计划是否成功。 此外，来自 CRM 解决方案的实时仪表板和报告用于比较过去和当前的活动，提供有关下游患者收入的有意义的统计数据，并将行动和收入与特定客户和提供商联系起来。

是什么让 CRM 系统可靠以及如何选择一个系统？

选择适合医院需求的 CRM 系统至关重要。 因此，医疗保健组织必须寻求提供专业 CRM 解决方案和 CRM 定制服务的专家的帮助。

选择供应商是一个挑战。 因此，检查参考资料、仔细听开场介绍、研究案例研究、与潜在供应商会面，然后才做出决定。

此外，重要的是要记住实施医疗保健 CRM 解决方案，无论是针对特定行业（如 Truven、Healthgrades）还是横向（如 Salesforce、Microsoft Dynamics CRM），都会对组织的所有领域和职能产生影响，全面的行政支持将大有裨益。



原文:

The demand for customer relationship management (CRM) software solutions is increasing across all types of businesses. Businesses of all sizes, small and large, as well as non-profits, use them to improve their services and increase collaboration. Information about customers and interactions is organized with CRM software and all business processes are streamlined.
Health care can be approached similarly. Overall, hospitals are adding multi-purpose CRM systems to solve a variety of problems faced by patients and physicians. Hospitals have different needs than commercial enterprises, but in general, they are implementing CRM systems to serve a variety of purposes.

Aspects to consider
As a result of CRM software adoption, hospitals are able to implement more successful marketing campaigns.

  1. Integrate data from across the healthcare facility.
  2. Analyse the database for the best marketing opportunities and methods of implementation.
  3. Measure the return on investment (ROI) of campaigns.

Major advantages
Campaigns to follow up with patients
The following contact models are enabled by CRM software: "doctor-patient," "doctor-doctor," "hospital-partner," and "hospital-customer" (e.g., managing the database of employed or potential doctors).

Thank you to modern technologies, healthcare organizations are now in the era of successful patient follow-up. CRM systems automate the sending of emails and text messages to remind patients to take their medications, schedule appointments, or communicate other important information.

What makes this message special is that while it's not as personal as a phone call, it's more convenient for hospitals and more likely to reach patients when it matters most. In addition, outside care is firmly guaranteed in this way.

Communication with the Individual Patient

CRM systems allow not only for sending reminders about appointments or taking medications but also for outbound communication. Patients can use chat to provide information about their current complaints, keep track of their appointments, and request changes if necessary.

These chats, messages, and appointments can be linked to the main dashboard, providing complete access to information about medical history, relationships with physicians, prescriptions, and more.

Communication with the hospital's management

CRM software also provides the ability to facilitate interaction between physicians. They have access to patient information that is essential for understanding new problems and drawing conclusions about each visit. This information includes medical tests, diagnoses, and previous treatments by other physicians. CRM conversations allow physicians to share helpful knowledge, offer advice, and reach a consensus on the issue at hand (symptoms, imaging results, etc.). The CRM software also enhances interaction with physicians through a variety of initiatives (physicians who meet certain criteria may attend certain visits.

Relationships between companies and physicians (B2P)

Hospitals also use modern CRM software to maintain long-term relationships with influential physicians who are no longer part of the team but are still willing to serve their patients. In addition to databases that store patient and insider physician data, the software can also set up a dedicated repository for key players in the field to improve B2P communications.

Ways to increase efficiency

Another opportunity CRM systems offer is ROI measurement. Using this tool, healthcare organizations can monitor actual results and evaluate the success of a particular marketing initiative aimed at attracting new patients or raising public awareness of serious diseases. In addition, real-time dashboards and reports from CRM solutions are used to compare past and current campaigns, offer meaningful statistics on patient revenue downstream, and link actions and revenue to specific customers and providers.

What makes a CRM system reliable and how to choose one?

It is crucial to select a CRM system that is tailored to the needs of hospitals. Healthcare organizations must therefore seek the help of experts who offer specialized CRM solutions and CRM customization services.

A CRM vendor should provide a database specifically designed for healthcare, audience segmentation required to define and design the target audience for various campaigns, and reports that can answer any questions.

Choosing a vendor is a challenge. Therefore, check references, listen carefully to opening presentations, research case studies, meet with potential vendors, and only then make a decision.

In addition, it's important to remember that implementing a healthcare CRM solution, whether industry-specific (such as Truven, Healthgrades) or horizontal (such as Salesforce, Microsoft Dynamics CRM), will have an impact on all areas and functions of the organization, and full executive support will be of great benefit.
